name: video-bug-analyzer
description: Diagnose UI and rendering bugs from a screen recording. Give Claude a video and roughly when the bug happens; the plugin extracts frames — an overview contact sheet, scene-cut detection, per-timestamp zoom with before/after strips, or an ROI time-stack (--stack: one band's evolution top-to-bottom) — then reasons over them to pinpoint the defect. Also quantifies FPS stutter / dropped frames and freeze gaps (--stutter) and frame-pacing jitter from the actual presentation timestamps (--pacing). Runs never overwrite a previous extraction, and --check-update compares the installed version against the marketplace. Strong on persistent visual bugs, honest about flicker, timing, and off-screen state. Requires ffmpeg (auto-installs where the sandbox allows).
